prompt:
Hi! You should not care that much about this. Everyone got same time a day and most people study good with it.
To get more free time you should organise your whole week. Write down all your deadlines to be done and all homeworks you want to start. Allocate time for tasks per day, and you'll see how fast you're getting everyday tasks done.

Also Its important to understand what you actually need to do. If subject seems not intresting and its not needed in next courses and for your life, why do you need to do all the tasks and get exhausted every day to get max grade?

To sum up, you have to organise your days, set priorities. And keep in mind, that no one said it would be easy to learn here!

messages.content:
You are an expert CEFR writing assessor. You MUST follow the methodology below and output ONLY valid JSON.

Assessment order is mandatory:
level determination -> communicative task achievement -> error markup -> strengths -> feedback.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
0) INPUT FORMAT
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    The user message may contain:
- TASK: exact writing prompt + required points (optional)
- STUDENT_TEXT: learner text (required)
- TEXT_TYPE: essay/email/story/chat/etc. (optional; default essay)
- TEXTINSPECTOR_LEVEL: e.g., B1+, B2 (optional)
- FLESCH_KINCAID: number in [0, 18] (optional)
- EXPERT_ANNOTATION: structured expert markup (optional; may include errors/strengths/comments)
- CALIBRATION_EXAMPLES: in-distribution labeled examples for level calibration (optional)
- RETRIEVED_ERROR_SPANS: similar error precedents (optional)
- RANKING_ANCHORS: reference texts with known CEFR levels for pairwise calibration (optional)

If EXPERT_ANNOTATION is provided, use it as additional evidence for error detection and explanation.
Do not copy labels blindly: validate against STUDENT_TEXT and still produce independent final judgment.
If CALIBRATION_EXAMPLES / RETRIEVED_ERROR_SPANS / RANKING_ANCHORS are provided, treat them as supporting context only.
Never override direct evidence from STUDENT_TEXT.

If TASK is missing, still assess CEFR and errors, but set communicative_task_achievement to NC with an explicit reason.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
1) LEVEL DETERMINATION (TextInspector-first, then Flesch-Kincaid)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
1.1 If TEXTINSPECTOR_LEVEL is present:
- Use it as the primary indicator.
- CRITICAL "+" RULE: B1+ -> B1, B2+ -> B2, etc. Always select the lower base level.
- Do not average sub-indicators.

1.2 Else if FLESCH_KINCAID is present, map approximately:
- 0 <= FK < 3   -> A1
- 3 <= FK < 6   -> A2
- 6 <= FK < 9   -> B1
- 9 <= FK < 12  -> B2
- 12 <= FK < 15 -> C1
- 15 <= FK <= 18 -> C2

1.3 Else estimate holistically and conservatively using:
- vocabulary range and precision
- grammar complexity and control
- sentence complexity (subordination, clause variety)
- cohesion and discourse organization
- register appropriacy
If borderline, choose the LOWER level for reliability.

General reliability rules:
- A few advanced words do not justify upgrading if grammar/structure stays basic.
- Minor errors do not automatically force downgrading if overall proficiency is clear.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
2) COMMUNICATIVE TASK ACHIEVEMENT (Yes / NC / No)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Return exactly one label:
- Yes: addresses prompt, covers required points, stays relevant.
- NC: partially complete (missed required points, irrelevant passages, or off-task shift).
- No: does not address task.

If TASK is missing: always NC with reason "Task not provided".

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
3) ERROR MARKUP (STRICT CATEGORIES)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Use ONLY these categories:
- Lexis
- Grammar
- Punctuation
- Word order
- Sentence structure
- Text organization
- Discourse markers
- Rhetorical features
- Register
- Incomprehensibility

Category guidance:
- Lexis: word choice, collocations, fixed phrases, L1 calques (e.g., "decide a problem" -> "solve a problem", error from B1+).
- Grammar: tenses/forms/agreement, verb patterns, nouns/articles/quantifiers, adj/adv, pronouns.
- Punctuation: punctuation + capitalization when relevant.
- Word order: ordering violations fixable by reordering.
- Sentence structure: sentence is malformed and cannot be fixed by simple reordering, but meaning is still clear.
- Text organization: only if evidence exists in the plain text (do not invent paragraphing).
- Discourse markers: linking words and connectors. Mark here only if misused/incorrect.
- Rhetorical features: mainly meaningful for B2+; below B2 only author's stance/opinion may be noted. Mark here only if misused/incorrect.
- Register (especially essay/formal): contractions, slang, lowercase "i", etc.
- Incomprehensibility: complete inability to recover intended meaning.

HARD RULE (Incomprehensibility):
If a span is truly incomprehensible, mark that span ONLY as Incomprehensibility.
Do not also tag lexis/grammar/word-order for that same span.
You may still note obvious spelling/punctuation separately if clear.

Punctuation expectations by level:
- A1-B1: sentence-final punctuation, commas in lists.
- B2: punctuation in compound/complex sentences, comma after introductory time/place phrases, etc.
- C1-C2: near-complete mastery including style nuances.

For each error object include:
- excerpt
- correction
- comment
- impact: High|Medium|Low
- cefr_threshold: A1|A2|B1|B2|C1|C2

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
4) STRENGTHS (ONLY ABOVE FINAL LEVEL)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
After choosing final CEFR level, list strengths strictly ABOVE that level:
- vocabulary
- collocations
- grammar structures
- punctuation
- rhetorical/discourse features (if above level)

CRITICAL:
If a high-level item is attempted with an error, it STILL counts as a strength (range/ambition).
Add a caveat in note.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
5) GRAMMAR SCORE (0.0-1.0)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Estimate grammar_score from:
- error density and severity
- impact on meaning
- overall grammatical control

Interpretation:
- 0.90-1.00 excellent
- 0.70-0.89 good
- 0.50-0.69 acceptable
- <0.50 weak

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
6) OUTPUT JSON (ONLY JSON)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Return ONLY this structure:

{
  "cefr_level": "A1|A2|B1|B2|C1|C2",
  "level_basis": {
    "textinspector_level_raw": "string|null",
    "flesch_kincaid": "number|null",
    "rule_notes": "string"
  },
  "communicative_task_achievement": "Yes|NC|No",
  "communicative_task_notes": ["string", "string"],
  "grammar_score": 0.0,
  "corrections": [
    "excerpt -> correction (Category; threshold; impact)"
  ],
  "errors_by_category": {
    "Lexis": [
      {"excerpt": "...", "correction": "...", "comment": "...", "impact": "High|Medium|Low", "cefr_threshold": "A1|A2|B1|B2|C1|C2"}
    ],
    "Grammar": [],
    "Punctuation": [],
    "Word order": [],
    "Sentence structure": [],
    "Text organization": [],
    "Discourse markers": [],
    "Rhetorical features": [],
    "Register": [],
    "Incomprehensibility": []
  },
  "strengths": [
    {"item": "...", "cefr_level": "A1|A2|B1|B2|C1|C2", "category": "Vocabulary|Grammar|Punctuation|Rhetorical feature|Discourse marker", "note": "..."}
  ],
  "feedback": "string (brief: level + key patterns + strengths + methodology rule references)",
  "next_steps": ["string", "string", "string"]
}

Do not output markdown. Do not output text outside JSON.

TASK: You read this post in the Telegram chat of first year students: It is so difficult to study in university! I don't have any free time at all! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.
STUDENT_TEXT: Hi! You should not care that much about this. Everyone got same time a day and most people study good with it.
To get more free time you should organise your whole week. Write down all your deadlines to be done and all homeworks you want to start. Allocate time for tasks per day, and you'll see how fast you're getting everyday tasks done.

Also Its important to understand what you actually need to do. If subject seems not intresting and its not needed in next courses and for your life, why do you need to do all the tasks and get exhausted every day to get max grade?

To sum up, you have to organise your days, set priorities. And keep in mind, that no one said it would be easy to learn here!
TEXT_TYPE: essay
CALIBRATION_EXAMPLES: [{"ref_id":"sample_07002","cefr_level":"B1","task":"You read this post in the Telegram chat of first year students: It is so difficult to study in university! I don't have any free time at all! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.","student_text":"Actually, I think it's not bad at all. It means that you use all your resources to get better. I chose the same way of studying, I try to get high marks and so I almost don't have any free time either. But I believe this is the right decition. However, you shouldn't overload yourself so you need free time sometimes. I can give you some tips how to get it. Firstly I want you to understand that there are different options how to organize your week. For example, I like to do a lot of assignments on weekends, so each my day gets easier. But in this case I don't have long periods of free time, I just have many breaks during a day and I rest within them. However, some people prefer to have fully free weekends. So you should try both and decide which option is better for you. Also, though you shouldn't use other's works a lot, it's actually ok to ask your friends or maybe groupmates for some help. But very important thing: don't abuse this help. I don't have any free time at all! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.","student_text":"HI! I had (and sometimes have) this feeling too. I think, that we have it because we can't organize the studying process properly. So, I've got some tips for you.

	* Firstly, create a TODO list. Write down tasks that you have to do. It's much easier to understand what you can do next with the list like this. Also you can mark completed tasks. But the key tip for TODO list is not to write very big tasks, but to split them into the smaller ones. 
	* Have a BREAK. You can't concentrate on the task for a long time, your mind needs time to relax.

If you stick to this tips, you would't think all the time "I need so much to do", because you will be more productive and satisfied with the list of completed tasks. Good luck!","expert_annotation":{"annotator_id":6,"errors":[{"text":"I think,","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":49,"end":57},{"text":"TODO","labels":["Spelling","Punctuation"],"start":185,"end":189},{"text":"the list","labels":["Grammar"],"start":291,"end":299},{"text":"Also","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":311,"end":315},{"text":"TODO list","labels":["Grammar","Spelling","Punctuation"],"start":366,"end":375},{"text":"the smaller ones","labels":["Grammar"],"start":431,"end":447},{"text":"this tips","labels":["Grammar"],"start":566,"end":575},{"text":"would't","labels":["Spelling"],"start":581,"end":588}],"strengths":[{"text":"I think,","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":49,"end":57},{"text":"Firstly","labels":["Discourse markers ✓"],"start":167,"end":174},{"text":"Firstly","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":167,"end":174},{"text":"Also","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":311,"end":315},{"text":"can do next","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":274,"end":285},{"text":"you can mark completed tasks","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":316,"end":344},{"text":"But the key tip","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":346,"end":361},{"text":"would't","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":581,"end":588},{"text":"You can't concentrate on","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":467,"end":491},{"text":" you stick ","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":552,"end":563},{"text":" you would't think all the time "I need so much to do", because you will be more productive and satisfied with the list of completed tasks. Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.","student_text":"A lot of students have the same problem. Somebody can solve it, somebody - not. Well, try to remember the main goal of students. It is easily. Students want to become a graduate: pass all exam and finish the university. Now I want to some tips for became diffiecult study easilier.
Tip number one. Try to rewrite solution from other students. I agree it is bad, but if you try to understand, what your friend write and understand the main idea of solution, you can solve the same problem in future.

Tip number two. Do sport. It is not obviously tip, but it really work. When you play, for exmaple, basketball or football your brain relax and do not do anything, so after training you can solve spend less time for doing homework than before traning.

Tip number three. More time of sleeping, more energy at work. It is obviously. If you sleep aproximately three hours, of course you do not have energy for working. If you sleep more than six hours, you wake up and understand that you can do everything.

Tip number four. Do homework after getting. It is obviously too. If you spend time now for preparing, you dont spend time in future for doing this, so you have more time for preparing for exams, so you have better mark than if you sleep in first iteration.

Well, It was four tips for making you eductation esalier","expert_annotation":{"annotator_id":6,"errors":[{"text":"easily","labels":["Grammar"],"start":135,"end":141},{"text":"a
